findings.summary: V78E05-500-SMT DC-DC converter (PS1) classified as 'connector' not a power supply type; Three of four RC filters at board level have incorrect or nonsensical net assignments due to cross-sheet topo...

correct.description: Unit 1 (inverting, Rf=R12 10k, Ri=R10 1k): gain=−10, 20 dB. Unit 2 (inverting, Rf=R2 30k, Ri=R1 100k): gain=−0.3, −10.5 dB. Unit 3 (inverting, Rf=R6 30k, Ri=R5 100k): gain=−0.3, −10.5 dB. Unit 4 (buffer, inv=out, non-inv=GND): gain=1.0. All verified by manual calculation.

total_components=50 is correct (50 unique reference designators). component_types: resistor=15 (R1–R12, R14, R15, RV1), led=2 (D1, D4), ic=4 (U1–U4), capacitor=19 (C1–C19), connector=5 (J1, J3, J4, J6, PS1 — though PS1 is misclassified), ferrite_bead=2 (FB1, FB2), diode=2 (D2, D3), speaker=1 (LS1). Sum = 50. The components list has 55 entries due to U3 appearing 5 times (4 op-amp units + power unit) and RV1 twice (dual-gang pot), both correctly deduplicated in statistics.

incorrect.description: PS1 is a CUI V78E05-500-SMT non-isolated DC-DC converter (6.5–36 V in → 5 V/500 mA out). Its symbol has a power_out pin and its description explicitly reads 'Dc-Dc Converter', yet the analyzer assigns type='connector' in both the BOM and component records. It should be classified as a power supply or regulator. This causes the component_types count to show 5 connectors (4 real + 1 misclassified) and masks the real connector count of 4.

The board-level analysis merges nets across all sub-sheets. Three of the four detected RC filters have net assignments that do not represent real signal paths: (1) R14+C9 shows output_net='+5V' and ground_net='__unnamed_27' with input_net='/…/IN_L' — a 1.59 Hz RC-network with a power rail as output is physically incorrect; (2) R15+C16 shows C16 as '2 caps parallel' (C16 + C9) but C9 is in a different sub-sheet; (3) R7+C9 shows ground_net='/…/IN_L' (an audio signal net used as ground). Only the R15+C16 low-pass entry in the headphone-amp sub-sheet (fc=15.92 Hz, input=SWITCHED_L, ground=GND) is correctly identified.

The analyzer warns that '+12V' and '-12V' have 'power_in pins but no power_out or PWR_FLAG'. However, J1 is an Eurorack 10-pin power header that provides these rails from an external power supply. In Eurorack designs the power connector itself supplies these rails and KiCad's ERC does not actually require a PWR_FLAG when the source is a connector with passive pins. The warning is an over-aggressive heuristic for connector-powered designs.

The design_analysis.differential_pairs entry for SPEAKER+/SPEAKER- sets has_esd=true and esd_protection=['U4']. U4 is the MAX9717x bridge-tied-load (BTL) mono speaker amplifier that drives SPEAKER+ and SPEAKER- as its outputs. It is the signal source, not an ESD protection device. There is no actual ESD protection on the speaker lines.

suggestions: Fix: V78E05-500-SMT DC-DC converter (PS1) classified as 'connector' not a power supply type

findings.summary: V78E05-500-SMT DC-DC converter not detected in power_regulators

missed.description: PS1 (V78E05-500-SMT) has a power_out pin type in its KiCad symbol and is a switching regulator converting +12 V → +5 V. The signal_analysis.power_regulators list is empty in both the power sub-sheet and the top-level board analysis. The power budget therefore cannot account for PS1 as a supply source for the +5 V rail.

findings.summary: Board outline not detected because the Edge.Cuts boundary is defined as a gr_poly rather than gr_line segments; board_width_mm and board_height_mm are null due to missed gr_poly Edge.Cuts

missed.description: The faceplate PCB defines its board outline using a single gr_poly element on the Edge.Cuts layer (coordinates: (124.4, 45.0) to (154.4, 173.5), giving a 30.0 × 128.5 mm panel). The analyzer only processes gr_line and gr_arc for edge detection and misses gr_poly, so it reports edge_count=0, bounding_box=null, and board_width_mm=null / board_height_mm=null. The board is a standard Eurorack 3U faceplate.

As a direct consequence of the gr_poly Edge.Cuts miss, statistics.board_width_mm and statistics.board_height_mm are null. The actual Eurorack faceplate is 30.0 mm wide and approximately 128.5 mm tall (3U panel height).

findings.summary: net_count=91 reads the KiCad 5 'general.nets' header value rather than counting nets used by pads

incorrect.description: The KiCad 5 PCB header (kicad_pcb … (general … (nets 92))) stores the next available net number, not the count of named nets in use. The source file declares only 5 unique net numbers (0, 78, 85, 86, 91) with corresponding names. The analyzer reports net_count=91, which appears to be 92 − 1 from the header value. The actual count of routed/used nets is 5 (or 4 excluding unconnected net 0). This inflated count misrepresents the board's complexity.

findings.summary: Board statistics correctly reflect the 4-layer 30×53.5 mm Eurorack board; GND zone spanning all four copper layers correctly identified and reported; Decoupling capacitor placement correctly identi...

correct.description: footprint_count=54 (50 real + 2 unnamed + G*** + REF** graphical), copper_layers_used=4 (F.Cu, In1.Cu, In2.Cu, B.Cu), track_segments=306, via_count=98, total_track_length_mm=555.07, board dimensions 30×53.5 mm, routing_complete=true, unrouted=0. All verified against source.

The zone analysis correctly identifies a single GND fill zone covering F.Cu, In1.Cu, In2.Cu, and B.Cu with connect_pads=thru_hole_only, clearance=0.2mm, thermal_gap=0.2mm, thermal_bridge_width=0.5mm. Filled areas per layer are accurately reported (F.Cu: 747 mm², In1.Cu: 1124 mm², In2.Cu: 1124 mm², B.Cu: 982 mm²).

U1 (MAX97220C): closest cap 3.4 mm, 12 nearby. U2 (74LVC1G04): closest 4.2 mm, 9 nearby. U3 (TL074): closest 4.3 mm (C10/C11 decoupling), 9 nearby. U4 (MAX9717x): closest 3.3 mm, 6 nearby. All ICs have correctly identified nearby decoupling capacitors.

findings.summary: Headphone breakout PCB dimensions, net count and routing correctly reported

correct.description: Board dimensions 16.5 × 19.5 mm verified against Edge.Cuts coordinates in source. net_count=5 correctly excludes the unnamed net 0. footprint_count=2 (FFC connector + 3.5 mm jack, both THT), 10 track segments, 1 GND zone, routing_complete=true. All verified against source.

findings.summary: Speaker circuit correctly identifies MAX9717x (U4) as driver IC for LS1

correct.description: buzzer_speaker_circuits entry correctly identifies LS1 (Speaker), its signal net SPEAKER+, and U4 (MAX9717x) as the driver IC. The MAX9717x is a BTL mono speaker amplifier, consistent with the identification.

findings.summary: Component counts and types accurate across 5 sub-sheets

correct.description: Total 50 components, 19 capacitors, 15 resistors, 4 ICs (U1–U4), 2 LEDs, 2 diodes, 2 ferrite beads, 1 speaker, 5 connectors, 1 potentiometer classified as resistor. All match the source. Hierarchical sheet traversal correctly includes all 5 sub-sheets (power, inputs, headphone-amp, speaker-amp, leds).

findings.summary: TL074 opamp circuit analysis: gains and configurations correct; Decoupling analysis correctly identifies -12V rail has no bulk capacitor; pwr_flag_warnings incorrectly fires for +12V and -12V — bot...

correct.description: U3 unit 1 (leds): inverting, gain=-10.0 (R12=10k / R10=1k), correct. U3 units 2 and 3 (inputs): inverting, gain=-0.3 (R2=30k / R1=100k and R6=30k / R5=100k), with 22pF feedback capacitors C18/C19 captured as feedback_capacitor, correct. U3 unit 4: buffer (output=inverting_input, GND on +), correct.
-12V rail decoupling shows has_bulk=false, cap_count=1 (only C11=100n). +12V has bulk (C2=22u) and bypass (C10=100n). +5V has good coverage. This is accurate and represents a real design asymmetry.

The sourcing_audit correctly shows 26/50 MPN coverage. The 24 missing-MPN components are all generic passives (most capacitors and some resistors) where the designer intentionally omitted MPNs. The components with MPNs are fully identified: all ICs (U1–U4), diodes (D2/D3), LEDs (D1/D4), ferrite beads (FB1/FB2), connectors (J1/J3/J4), PS1, and some resistors.

incorrect.description: The power.kicad_sch sheet contains two PWR_FLAG symbols: #FLG01 at (81.28, 100.33) placed on the +12V net, and #FLG02 at (81.28, 104.14) placed on the -12V net. Both flags are at the same Y coordinates as their respective power symbols. Despite this, pwr_flag_warnings reports both '+12V' and '-12V' as missing PWR_FLAG. The analyzer is failing to resolve the PWR_FLAG connections in the sub-sheet context.

The signal_analysis.rc_filters output includes an entry pairing R14 (100k) with C9 (1u), reporting type='RC-network' with cutoff=1.59 Hz and output_net='+5V'. In the actual circuit, R14 is a pull-up/bias resistor from SWITCHED_L to +5V; C9 is a decoupling/signal capacitor in the speaker amp sheet. They are not topologically connected as an RC filter pair. This is a cross-sheet net name collision artifact. The real RC filter on the headphone side is R15+C16 (correctly reported in the headphone-amp sub-sheet output as low-pass at 15.92 Hz).

The V78E05-500-SMT is a CUI 500mA non-isolated DC-DC step-down converter producing +5V from the +12V Eurorack rail. It is classified as type='connector' because the symbol lib_id is a custom winterbloom library entry. It should be 'ic' or 'regulator'. Consequently, the power_regulators section of signal_analysis is empty — the +12V→+5V conversion is not detected.

LS1 (speaker) is driven by MAX9717x (U4), a class-D BTL audio power amplifier. The output reports has_transistor_driver=false and direct_gpio_drive=true, but U4 is an IC amplifier, not a GPIO. The SPEAKER+ and SPEAKER- nets both connect from U4 to LS1, indicating a differential/BTL topology that is not captured. direct_gpio_drive=true is incorrect.

TL074 is a quad op-amp: units 1–4 are op-amp sections, unit 5 is the VCC/VEE power pin unit. The output lists unit 5 under opamp_circuits with configuration='unknown'. This unit should not appear as an op-amp circuit entry; it is the power supply connectivity unit for the IC. This produces noise in the opamp_circuits list.

missed.description: The leds sub-sheet uses a back-to-back anti-parallel LED arrangement: D1 (cathode) and D4 (anode) share net __unnamed_1 (U3A output), while D1 (anode) and D4 (cathode) share net __unnamed_2 (to GND via R8). This is a bidirectional LED peak indicator driven by the opamp output. The analyzer does not detect or label this as an anti-parallel / peak indicator LED circuit; it appears only in the raw ic_pin_analysis without functional characterization.

U2 (74LVC1G04 NOT gate) in headphone-amp inverts the ~HEADPHONE_PRESENT signal (from J6 normalling contact) through an R15+C16 debounce RC filter, producing SHDN to mute the speaker amplifier U4 when headphones are inserted. This is the core analog headphone detect / speaker mute circuit. The analyzer does not connect these pieces into a signal path description. The signal U2 output (~{HEADPHONE_PRESENT}) appears as a single_pin_net design_observation rather than being characterized as an enable/disable control path.

findings.summary: 4-layer board fully routed, dimensions, stackup, and routing stats accurate; Thermal pad vias on U4 (MAX9717x) correctly identified as adequate; Board-only footprints (OSHW logo, custom logos, grap...

correct.description: Board correctly identified as 4-layer (F.Cu, In1.Cu, In2.Cu, B.Cu), 30×53.5mm (standard Eurorack 6HP), 54 footprints (50 BOM + 4 board-only graphics/logos), 306 track segments, 98 vias, 1 GND zone, routing_complete=true, unrouted=0. Stackup details including dielectric thicknesses and FR4 properties are correctly parsed.

U4 TDFN-8 thermal pad (9, 3.82mm², GND net) has 5 standalone vias (effective 8.9 with GND pour fill), density 2.33/mm², adequacy='adequate'. The tenting warning (5 untented vias may cause solder wicking) is also correctly flagged. The recommended minimum of 5 vias is met.

The 4 non-BOM footprints (OSHW-Logo, Winterbloom LOGO, 2× Graphics) are all flagged with board_only=true, exclude_from_bom=true, exclude_from_pos=true, pad_count=0. The BOM footprint count is 50, matching the schematic's 50 components.

The placement_analysis reports 14 courtyard overlaps. The largest (70.09mm²) between RV1 (dual potentiometer) and PS1 (DC-DC converter) is verified as real by coordinate calculation: RV1 courtyard [75.84–88.56, 115.1–131.35] vs PS1 [72.65–85.35, 123.98–139.72] gives x_overlap=9.51mm, y_overlap=7.37mm = 70.1mm². These are design-intentional overlaps common in Eurorack modules where 3D clearances are managed manually.

PCB has 43 nets total, 40 with routing segments, 3 without: unconnected-(PS1-Adj-Pad6), unconnected-(PS1-EN-Pad10), and unconnected-(U2-NC-Pad1). These are intentional no-connect pads: PS1's Adj and EN pins are unused on the V78E05-500-SMT, and U2's (74LVC1G04) NC pin is by definition unconnected. routing_complete=true is correctly reported.

incorrect.description: The connectivity block reports total_nets_with_pads=43, routed_nets=40, unrouted_count=0. The discrepancy (43-40=3) is because 3 nets have only one pad each (the no-connect stubs above). These are not unrouted but rather single-pad nets. The field name 'routed_nets' is misleading — it counts nets that have routing segments, excluding single-pad nets. The combination of unrouted_count=0 and routed_nets≠total_nets is confusing but not strictly wrong given the single-pad explanation.

missed.description: The board is a standard Eurorack 3U module (30mm width = 6HP, 53.5mm height ≈ 3U). The PCB analyzer reports the raw dimensions but does not classify this as a Eurorack module or verify against the expected Eurorack PCB profile (standard is 30mm wide, height typically 53.5–60mm for 3U modules). The board_metadata section does not contain form-factor classification.
